_question: Rewrite scene_M1.json v5: integrate set_list_M1 v5 (existing-asset backgrounds + competitors_in_air overlay) and pose_composition_map v5 (run -> stand -> catch -> jump -> land). Resolve Nirit's three v4 complaints: (a) bg was empty gradient not sky/plane, (b) Nano Banana not allowed, (c) post-click 'floating' before landing. This is the final attempt before fallback to static slideshow.

rounds.voice_a: **Position A1 — Sky readability + zero new assets.** v4 failed on Nirit's first complaint because the bg was a CSS gradient that read as 'a blue/gold rectangle' — not as 'sky'. set_manager v5 already solved this by pointing Phase 1 at C:/emerald/backgrounds/bg_airplane.mp4 (real photoreal aerial sky+canopy, verified on disk, despite the misleading filename). My job in scene_brief v5 is to STOP overriding that with CSS gradients. So scene_brief v5 background.phase_1 must be: type=video, file=C:/emerald/backgrounds/bg_airplane.mp4, fit=cover, loop=true, z=0. Drop the entire css_gradient_with_motion block and its three cloud_streak layers — they were the v4 mistake. Same for Phase 2: replace bg_M5.mp4 with set_list's bg_jungle_clearing.mp4 (ground-POV jungle floor matching pose_03 hands-on-ground crouch). NO Nano Banana, NO new generation, scene_brief just REFERENCES set_list_M1's decisions.
rounds.voice_b: **Challenge B1 — agreed in principle; but flag the photo-realistic aerial bg behind a chroma-keyed runner.** I accept dropping the gradient. Concern: Nirit will see pose_04 (profile run on green) chroma-keyed onto bg_airplane.mp4 (aerial sky over canopy) and ask 'why is she running on AIR with no platform under her feet?' The 'running' was Nirit-approved in v4 *because* the bg was vague gradient — running across abstract sky reads as 'mid-jump action shot'. Now bg is concrete altitude POV — a person running in mid-air over jungle treetops looks broken. We need EITHER (a) keep her at bottom:8% so her feet are below the visible canopy edge (looks like she's at the door of an unseen plane), or (b) reduce running emphasis (set_list already cuts it from loop to one_shot 1500ms — that may be enough). I lean (a) + (b) combined.
rounds.moderator_summary: Both voices agree: drop the CSS gradient, use bg_airplane.mp4 + bg_jungle_clearing.mp4 (existing files, verified). Open issue: how to reconcile profile-running pose against a real aerial background without 'running on air' breaking visual logic. Voice B's combo solution (low CSS position + short run duration + immediate transition to standing) is on the table. Voice A to address in round 2.

rounds.voice_a: **Position A2 — Yes, low position + short run + facing left.** Voice B is right. Solution: position_css for phase_1_running_entry = 'bottom:6%; right:55%; height:54%; transform:scaleX(-1)'. Three things together: (1) bottom:6% drops her low — her feet are below the bottom edge of bg_airplane.mp4's visible canopy, so the eye doesn't 'see ground under her not matching'. The illusion is 'she just stepped out — we don't see what's under her feet'. (2) right:55% places her LEFT of center (entering from right edge implied — Hebrew RTL reads right-to-left, so 'entering scene' = appearing on the right and moving left). transform:scaleX(-1) flips pose_04 (which is right-facing default per pose_map evidence) so she faces LEFT — into the wind, into the jump. (3) height:54% (slightly under design_system.md desktop 52vh max-height-420px, fits). The 1500ms one_shot run from set_list/pose_composition v5 is already short — no time for the eye to register 'running on air'. Then immediate cross-fade to pose_05 grounded standing at the same position — by the time the eye locks on, she's standing.
rounds.voice_b: **Challenge B2 — accepted. One refinement: standing position must NOT translate from running position.** If running_entry is bottom:6% right:55% and pose_05 standing_wait switches to a different position_css, builder will animate the canvas across the screen during the 200ms cross-fade and Nirit will see the player slide. Nirit verbatim from v4: 'היא מרחפת לה שם כמה שניות טובות, בעמידה מוחלטת ואז פתאום נעצרת על הקרקע' — she rejected exactly that 'sliding/floating in standing position' read. Lock pose_05 standing_wait to the SAME position_css as pose_04 running_entry (bottom:6%, right:55%, height:54%). Only the pose video reference changes; the CSS wrapper stays put. After click (catch_one_shot), same position. After catch_complete (jump_css_overlay) — CSS animates UP via translateY, NOT a position swap. After jump_apex (landing) — position_css changes to bottom:5% right:30% height:55% as the canvas CSS-translates DOWN over the 2000ms landing duration. ONE position swap for the entire scene, at landing.
rounds.moderator_summary: Strong convergence. The single-position rule (running, standing, catching, jumping all share bottom:6% right:55% height:54%; only the landing changes to bottom:5% right:30% height:55%) is aligned with Nirit's anti-floating constraint. Open: facing direction during catch (frontal vs profile), since pose_06 is frontal-reach but the player is currently flipped left-facing for run. Voice A to address.

rounds.voice_a: **Position A3 — Catch flips back to frontal; flip removed at the run->stand seam.** pose_05 segment 1.0-2.0 (per actor_director v5) is a frontal grounded stance — flipping it would mirror her hair-tie/backpack and break character consistency. So at the moment of the run->stand cross-fade (= mission_text_shown event = scene_entry+1500ms), the wrapper transform changes from scaleX(-1) to scaleX(1). The 200ms blend hides the flip. From standing onward (pose_05, pose_06 catch, pose_06 jump-hold) — all frontal, transform:scaleX(1). pose_03 landing — frontal, transform:scaleX(1). One flip in the scene, hidden by the cross-fade at t=1500ms. Catch trajectory then makes sense: tool flies from a zone-D button (bottom:80%-ish, screen center+stagger) on a parabolic arc up-and-to-the-player's-palm. Player at right:55% means palm is around screen-x ≈ 45% (0.55 anchor + ~0.10 offset for raised right hand at frame 4.5). Tool palm-target pixel-coords resolved by builder via getBoundingClientRect on the player canvas at click moment.
rounds.voice_b: **Challenge B3 — accepted. Two final concerns:** (1) The tool flight cubic-bezier should arc UPWARD before descending into palm — not a straight line — to sell 'tossed to her', not 'sucked in'. set_list has the dust_clouds at jump_apex+1800ms (= landing moment) which works, but I want to flag: NO dust during catch (catch happens in air, not ground), NO dust during jump. (2) Rivals — set_list_M1 v5 uses competitors_in_air.png as a chroma-keyed PNG overlay in upper sky. v4 had 5 CSS dots. Voice A's draft must DROP the rivals[] CSS-dots block and replace with a single overlay reference to set_list's scenery_overlays[0]. The competitors_in_air.png contains six rival figures already — meets the 'felt-not-seen' rule with opacity:0.78 + blur(0.4px) + slow drift. Don't double-rivals.
rounds.moderator_summary: All three concerns resolved. Tool arc parabolic (cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1) for arc-up-then-down). No dust during catch/jump. Rivals = single PNG overlay from set_list, drop CSS dots. Ready for round 4 (final synthesis).

rounds.voice_a: **Final position A — v5 scene_brief shape locked.** Background.phase_1 = video bg_airplane.mp4. Background.phase_2 = video bg_jungle_clearing.mp4. bg_swap on jump_apex with 400ms crossfade (set_list spec — tightened from v4's 500ms). Player single-position: bottom:6% right:55% height:54% scaleX(-1) for run, scaleX(1) for everything from standing onward, EXCEPT landing which CSS-translates over 2000ms to bottom:5% right:30% height:55%. Five pose tracks per actor_director v5: pose_04 #2.0-3.5 one_shot (run), pose_05 #1.0-2.0 loop (stand), pose_06 #3.0-5.5 one_shot (catch), pose_06 #5.5 hold (jump-css-overlay), pose_03 #3.0-5.0 one_shot is_landing (land). Tool flight 1500ms arc cubic-bezier(0.34,1.56,0.64,1). Mission text fade in at t=1500ms (fires mission_text_shown event same RAF tick). Tools fade in at t=2500ms (1s after mission per Nirit verbatim). Catch fires on tool_clicked. catch_complete at click+2500ms. jump_apex at catch_complete+300ms. landing_complete at jump_apex+2000ms. Checkpoint text at landing_complete+200ms. Rivals = competitors_in_air.png from set_list (single overlay, no CSS dots). Scenery phase 1 = none extra (just rivals overlay). Scenery phase 2 = two_jungle_trees.png + dust_clouds.png (timed dust at jump_apex+1800ms). NO Nano Banana. NO new generation.
rounds.voice_b: **Final position B — approved with one explicit builder-flag.** A's plan integrates set_list and pose_composition_map cleanly. One must-flag: the v4 had tools_first_appear_ms=2000 (1s after mission at 1500ms). pose_composition v5's _timeline_summary states 't=2000ms tools_first_appear'. But the user/Nirit's verbatim says 'שנייה אחרי המשימה הכלים מופיעים' — 'a second after the mission, tools appear'. Mission_text_shown event fires when fade_in BEGINS at t=1500ms; the mission text becomes visually settled around t=1900ms (after 400ms fade). 'A second after the mission' is ambiguous — does Nirit mean +1000ms after fade-in start (t=2500ms) or +1000ms after the mission is fully readable (t=2900ms)? I lean on 2500ms (1s after mission_text_shown event) as the tighter reading. Builder must pull `tools_first_appear_ms = mission_text_appears_ms + 1000` from timing block. Also lock rivals overlay z-index strictly: bg=0, rivals=3 (per set_list), scenery_trees=4, player=10, tools_in_container=20, mission/checkpoint=30, dust=15. No drift from design_system.md layer-stack.
rounds.moderator_summary: {"synthesis": "v5 scene_M1.json: REFERENCES (does not override) set_list_M1 v5 + pose_composition_map v5. Background.phase_1 = video bg_airplane.mp4 (existing file, real aerial sky+canopy, NO css gradient, NO Nano Banana). Background.phase_2 = video bg_jungle_clearing.mp4 (existing, ground-POV). Crossfade at jump_apex over 400ms. Player single-position-anchor for the entire pre-landing arc: bottom:6% right:55% height:54%; transform=scaleX(-1) for pose_04 running 1500ms, transform=scaleX(1) from pose_05 standing onward (flip hidden inside the 200ms cross-fade at mission_text_shown). At jump_apex the player canvas CSS-translates over 2000ms to bottom:5% right:30% height:55% (landing target). Five pose tracks exactly per actor_director v5 — director DOES NOT modify them, only references. Timing: scene_entry t=0; mission_text_shown t=1500ms (200ms after pose_04 ends, 200ms blend covers the seam); tools_first_appear t=2500ms (= mission_text_shown + 1000ms per Nirit verbatim 'שנייה אחרי המשימה'); tool_stagger_ms=120ms; wait_for_click; click+2500ms = catch_complete; +300ms = jump_apex (bg crossfade starts); +2000ms = landing_complete; +200ms = checkpoint text fade-in (400ms). Tool flight 1500ms cubic-bezier(0.34,1.56,0.64,1) parabolic arc from clicked-button-rect to player-palm-rect (computed at click time). Rivals = single competitors_in_air.png overlay from set_list (drop the v4 CSS dots block). Scenery phase 2 = two_jungle_trees.png + dust_clouds.png (dust trigger at jump_apex+1800ms only, NO dust at catch or jump). Z-stack: bg=0, scenery_trees=2, rivals=3, player=10, tools_in_container=20, mission/checkpoint=30, effects/dust=15. tool_consequences A=deploy (parachute opens above), B=wear (glider strapped), C=wear (wingsuit). NO pose_07 (rejected v3/v4). NO idle floating between catch and landing (pose_06 brief 2500ms catch + 600ms jump = 3100ms total before pose_03 landing starts). pose_05 1.0-2.0 frontal-grounded segment ONLY in pre-click wait window. ONE bg crossfade, ONE flip, ONE position translate. Builder contract reaffirmed: video.loop=false, RAF-clamped segments, separate <video> elements per pose, fastSeek/loadedmetadata for from_sec, NEVER reassign .src.", "decision": "pass", "director_flag": false, "flag_reason": null, "fix_notes": ""}

final_decision.synthesis: scene_M1.json v5 fully integrates set_list_M1 v5 (existing-asset backgrounds: bg_airplane.mp4 phase 1, bg_jungle_clearing.mp4 phase 2, plus competitors_in_air.png chroma-keyed overlay, two_jungle_trees.png, dust_clouds.png) and pose_composition_map v5 (5 pose tracks: pose_04 #2.0-3.5 one_shot run, pose_05 #1.0-2.0 loop stand, pose_06 #3.0-5.5 one_shot catch, pose_06 #5.5 hold jump-css-overlay, pose_03 #3.0-5.0 one_shot is_landing land). Solves Nirit's three v4 rejections: (1) bg now real photoreal aerial sky video, not gradient; (2) zero Nano Banana / Veo / Leonardo invocation, every file path verified on disk; (3) NO floating between catch and landing — pose_06 catch (2500ms) + CSS jump (600ms) directly into pose_03 landing (2000ms), with single position translate at jump_apex from bottom:6% right:55% height:54% to bottom:5% right:30% height:55%. Run -> stand transition uses single-position-anchor + transform flip hidden inside 200ms blend, eliminating Nirit's 'sliding standing' complaint. Tools appear 1000ms after mission_text per Nirit verbatim. Tool flight is 1500ms parabolic arc cubic-bezier(0.34,1.56,0.64,1). NO rivals as CSS dots — replaced with set_list's competitors_in_air.png overlay.
open_questions_for_downstream: Builder: confirm getBoundingClientRect-based palm-target resolution at tool_clicked moment for the parabolic arc — palm pixel position depends on the chroma-keyed canvas, which depends on the active <video> element's bounding rect at click time.

Sound designer: scene v5 has no sound spec block (out of director scope). M1 needs (a) wind ambient during run, (b) UI chime stagger on tool appear, (c) whoosh on tool flight, (d) soft thud + jingle at palm contact (click+1500ms), (e) impact + jungle ambient at landing_complete, (f) no audio sting during checkpoint fade-in.

Human review: validate pose_05 1.0-2.0 segment STILL reads as grounded after the scaleX flip transition — actor_director flagged fallback to is_pose_hold at frame 1.0 if the 1-second loop introduces motion that reads floaty.
